title: Throat clearing

examination.text: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.
examination.rationale: The exact adult chronic-cough section supports duration, throat-clearing or cough context, triggers, smoking, medicine, rhinitis, reflux, functional impact and upper-airway examination without assigning a cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

investigations.text: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.
investigations.rationale: The exact investigation context supports recording reviewed specialist information and a clinician-entered plan or follow-up without ordering tests or treatment.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults

source_limitations: Paediatric presentation, exact cause, laryngeal findings, infection, reflux, tic or habit context, diagnosis, and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
